{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents consistently mention that glaucomatous optic neuropathy involves the death of retinal ganglion cells. Document [0] states 'Glaucoma – the gradual degeneration of ganglion cells and their axons resulting in the loss of peripheral and, eventually, central vision.' Document [1] mentions 'The exact cause of glaucoma is not fully known, but the underlying pathology lies in the apoptosis of retinal ganglion cells.' Document [2] defines glaucoma as 'an acquired loss of retinal ganglion cells and axons within the optic nerve or optic neuropathy.' Document [8] describes 'Early in the disease process, glaucomatous optic nerve atrophy can present with thinning and atrophy of the retinal ganglion cell layer and, thus, thinning of the nerve fiber layer above the ganglion cells.' All these documents point to the ganglion cells being the primary cells affected in glaucomatous optic neuropathy.", "answer_choice": "C"}
